hook: You already know how to solve equations like $x + 3 = 7$ to find one exact answer. But what if you needed to find every number that makes your budget work, your speed legal, or your grade passing? This unit unlocks a powerful upgrade: instead of pinpointing a single value, you'll describe entire ranges of solutions.
concept: Every topic in this unit is about finding and expressing all values that satisfy a condition rather than a single exact answer — formally, solving and representing solution sets of linear inequalities over the real numbers.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn the rules that govern inequalities — including why multiplying or dividing by a negative number flips the inequality sign — so every future solving step has a solid foundation.
Apply a single operation to isolate the variable and write the solution set for inequalities like $x - 4 > 2$ or $3x \leq 12$.
Master solving inequalities that require two or more steps — combining like terms, distributing, and isolating the variable — just as you would with multi-step equations.
You will learn to solve and interpret two inequalities joined by "and" or "or," identifying whether solutions must satisfy both conditions simultaneously or at least one of them.
Apply your understanding of absolute value to rewrite and solve inequalities like $|2x - 1| < 5$, translating them into compound inequalities you already know how to handle.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Graphing Inequalities on a Number Line
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to represent any solution set visually on a number line — using open and closed circles and shaded rays or segments — so the meaning of your answer is instantly clear.
